differentials.name: surgical resection difficult because ACTH adenomas usually require resection of an apparently normal gland
differentials.raw: surgical resection difficult because ACTH adenomas usually require resection of an apparently normal gland (tumor small + usually not on surface of gland)

differentials.name: gigantism, acromegaly, elevated GH > 10 ng/mL, NO rise in GH after administration of
differentials.raw: gigantism, acromegaly, elevated GH > 10 ng/mL, NO rise in GH after administration of

differentials.name: GONADOTROPHIC CELL ADENOMA
differentials.raw: GONADOTROPHIC CELL ADENOMA (7%) secretes follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) / luteinizing hormone (LH)

differentials.name: THYROTROPHIC CELL ADENOMA
differentials.raw: THYROTROPHIC CELL ADENOMA (< 1%) secretes thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH)

surgical resection difficult because ACT: central location; posterior lobe; usually < 5 mm in size

context: Age:   30-40 years; M÷F = 1÷3 √  central location; posterior lobe; usually < 5 mm in size √  blood sampling of inferior petrosal sinuses (95% diagnostic accuracy compared with 65% for MR) Rx:        (1)  suppression by high doses of dexamethasone of 8 mg/d glucose / TRH Histo:   (a)   densely granulated type (b)   sparsely granulated type: more aggressive √  hypodense region, may be less well-defined, variable size √  slow-growing often extending beyond sella √  often large + invasive pituitary adenoma
